## ka

### Sense 1: lexicon

- **Pronunciation:** `KA`
- **Part of speech:** particle
- **Definition:** completed action
- **Register/status:** grammar; accepted
- **Domains:** daily-life
- **Literal:** grammatical particle: aspect
- **Example:** Lem ka mar.
- **Corpus:** observed (2 attestations); tracks: civic-law; examples in civic-019

### Sense 2: particle

- **Pronunciation:** `KA`
- **Part of speech:** particle:aspect
- **Definition:** completed action
- **Register/status:** aspect; accepted
- **Domains:** daily-life

- **Pronunciation:** `KANAC-RET`
- **Part of speech:** noun
- **Definition:** right, claim, or protected standing concerning: contract, binding agreement
- **Register/status:** civic; accepted
- **Domains:** law-governance, economics-provision, travel-place
- **Root family:** `KNC`; Law/civic life; contract, binding agreement; contract, binding agreement, enforceable exchange of promises
- **Pattern:** right
- **Literal:** K-N-C root, right suffix -ret
- **Example:** Kanac-ret ret cel sak.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)

## kanac-tel

### Sense 1: lexicon

- **Pronunciation:** `KANAC-TEL`
- **Part of speech:** noun
- **Definition:** instrument, tool, medium, or interface for: to contract, bind agreement, or enter enforceable exchange
- **Register/status:** technical; accepted
- **Domains:** law-governance, economics-provision, travel-place
- **Root family:** `KNC`; Law/civic life; contract, binding agreement; contract, binding agreement, enforceable exchange of promises
- **Pattern:** instrument
- **Literal:** K-N-C root, instrument suffix -tel
- **Example:** Na ban bi kanac-tel.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)

## kanacaf

### Sense 1: lexicon

- **Pronunciation:** `KANACAF`
- **Part of speech:** verb
- **Definition:** to conflict as kin, bring household dispute to truth, or repair family rupture
- **Register/status:** plain; accepted
- **Domains:** family-kinship
- **Root family:** `KNCF`; Family; kin conflict, household dispute; kin conflict, family rupture, household dispute needing truth and repair
- **Pattern:** verb
- **Literal:** K-N-C-F root, action vowel a
- **Example:** Na kanacaf.
- **Corpus:** active (4 attestations); tracks: civic-law, ritual-vow; examples in civic-1317, ritual-1317

- **Pronunciation:** `KANATAR-TEL`
- **Part of speech:** noun
- **Definition:** instrument, tool, medium, or interface for: to contraindicate, warn against treatment, or block harmful remedy
- **Register/status:** technical; accepted
- **Domains:** body-health
- **Root family:** `KNTR`; Body; contraindication, treatment warning; contraindication, warning against treatment, care-limiting danger
- **Pattern:** instrument
- **Literal:** K-N-T-R root, instrument suffix -tel
- **Example:** Na ban bi kanatar-tel.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)

## kanatax

### Sense 1: lexicon

- **Pronunciation:** `KANATAX`
- **Part of speech:** verb
- **Definition:** to feel contrition, grieve wrong, or turn guilt toward repair
- **Register/status:** plain; accepted
- **Domains:** emotion-psychology

- **Pattern:** verb
- **Literal:** K-N-T-X root, action vowel a
- **Example:** Na kanatax.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)
